title: Add trails feature: branch-centric work tracking
description:
Implements the trails feature for branch-centric work tracking. Trails are metadata objects stored on the entire/trails orphan branch that track the lifecycle of feature branches.

Core changes:
- trail package (cmd/entire/cli/trail/): Store with CRUD operations on entire/trails orphan branch, sharded storage (metadata.json, discussion.json, checkpoints.json per trail)
- trail commands: list, create, update with interactive and flag-based modes
- Interactive trail create: title-first flow that derives branch name via slugification, creates the git branch, pushes both branch and trail data to origin
- Interactive trail update: pre-filled form with current values when no flags provided
- Trail auto-creation on TurnStart for non-main branches without a trail
- Checkpoint linking after each condensation/save (appended to checkpoints.json, newest first)
- Pre-push hooks push entire/trails branch alongside entire/checkpoints/v1
- Trail title generation from first user prompt via TextGenerator agent interface
- Remote trail fetching before listing so collaborators' trails are visible
- GitHub username resolution for trail author (gh api user fallback to git user.name)
- Removed trail enable/disable commands (trails are always active)
- Status lifecycle: draft → open → in_progress → in_review → done → closed
author: Daniel Adams
